title: BIS Quarterly Review, December 2025
summary: The BIS Quarterly Review December 2025 presents comprehensive analysis of international banking and financial market developments, with particular focus on FX market structure following the April 2025 Triennial Survey, the continuing transition away from Libor in interest rate derivatives markets, and the growing role of basis trading strategies. The publication documents structural shifts including a surge in FX trading volumes driven by hedging activity amid global monetary policy uncertainty, and uneven progress in emerging market FX and derivatives market development.
key_points: The 2025 BIS Triennial Survey reveals significant shifts in FX market structure, with a surge in trading volumes in April 2025 driven by hedging activity around tariff news and monetary policy tightening.
Interest rate derivatives markets show structural transformation as benchmark reform continues, with the cash-futures basis trade and XTD positioning becoming more prominent.
Euro contracts are migrating from London to Germany following benchmark reform, representing a geographical shift in OTC IRD turnover.
EME (emerging market economy) FX and derivatives trading shows uneven progress, with renminbi propelling growth in EME currency trading.
The growth in OTC IRD markets for EME currencies faces remaining challenges including concentration of clearing.
The publication analyzes risk asset performance amid growing concerns, sovereign yield movements with monetary easing, and precious metals and USD appreciation.
why_it_matters: This BIS Quarterly Review provides essential market structure intelligence for practitioners, regulators, and researchers tracking the structural evolution of global FX and interest rate derivatives markets. The documentation of post-Libor market structure, the cash-futures basis trade dynamics, and the geographical shifts in derivatives trading (especially euro contracts migrating to Germany) are critical for understanding current market plumbing. The April 2025 FX volume surge data provides benchmark data for market participants assessing liquidity conditions and execution quality.
macro_context: The publication addresses macro developments including risk asset performance, sovereign yields, and precious metals alongside the structural analysis. Box A discusses tech stock performance differentials globally, Box B examines compressed credit spreads and risk-free rate dynamics, and Box C analyzes bubble conditions in US equities and gold. These macro observations are contextual rather than forward-looking.
market_structure_context: Core focus areas include: (1) FX market structure documented through 2025 Triennial Survey data including non-visible trading and execution landscape; (2) Interest rate derivatives market structure including OTC vs exchange-traded dynamics, Libor benchmark reform, and cash-futures basis trade mechanics; (3) Geographical shifts in turnover with euro contract migration to Germany; (4) Clearing concentration challenges for emerging market OTC IRD markets; (5) Relative value trades in US Treasuries and interest rate swaps analyzed in Box B.
